data.task:
Atom-0 — core repointTicket primitive (symmetric to createTicket/closeTicket). Build the governed write spine that repoints or clears a ticket's `priority:` frontmatter. This is verified-when #1 only; do NOT wire archive/reconciliation yet (that is a later atom).

WHAT TO BUILD
1. New file packages/core/src/tickets/repoint.ts exporting `repointTicket(input): Promise<RepointTicketResult>`, modeled on packages/core/src/tickets/close.ts and create.ts (same imports, same `unique`, same round-trip self-check pattern).
   - Input: { readonly ticketsDir: string; readonly repoPath: string; readonly ticketId: string; readonly targetPriority: string | null }. `null` (or the literal 'none') means RELEASE to standalone (clear). A non-empty priority slug means REHOME to that priority.
   - Behavior on an OPEN ticket: rewrite ONLY the `priority:` frontmatter line in the ticket file (release -> `priority: none`; rehome -> `priority: <target>`), AND update the Priority cell of that ticket's row in the `## Open` table of tickets/INDEX.md IN PLACE (the row stays in Open; only the Priority column changes).
   - HARD CONSTRAINTS (these are the point of the primitive): do NOT move the file out of open/, do NOT change `status:` (NO auto-close), do NOT touch order.json (no silent launch-queue mutation), do NOT alter any other frontmatter field or table cell. Mutation is `priority:` frontmatter + its INDEX Priority cell ONLY.
   - Result: discriminated union. Success: { repointed: true; files: readonly string[]; targetPriority: string | null }. No-op cases: { repointed: false; reason: 'missing-open-ticket' | 'already-at-target' } with files: []. Treat a closed or absent ticket as 'missing-open-ticket' (use findOpenTicketFile pattern from close.ts). 'already-at-target' when the open ticket's normalized priority already equals the requested target (normalize 'none'/'unassigned'/empty -> standalone, matching handled.ts normalizedTicketPriority semantics) — in that case write nothing.
   - End with a round-trip self-check like close.ts: re-read via readTickets, assert the ticket is still state==='open', status unchanged, and its priority matches the requested target (null/none for release); throw if it did not round-trip.

2. Add an index helper in packages/core/src/tickets/index-helpers.ts: `setOpenTicketIndexPriority(indexMarkdown: string, id: string, priority: string): string` that finds the ticket's row inside the `## Open` section (reuse sectionBounds) and replaces ONLY the Priority column cell (4th column: ID | Title | Type | Priority | Owner), preserving the other cells exactly. Throw if the id is not present in the Open section. Use ticketTableCell to escape the new value.

3. Export `repointTicket`, `RepointTicketInput`, `RepointTicketResult` from packages/core/src/tickets/index.ts, and add `setOpenTicketIndexPriority` to its index-helpers export block. Surface them through packages/core/src/index.ts the same way the other ticket symbols are surfaced.

TESTS (add to packages/core/tests/tickets.test.ts, matching its existing fixture style)
- release: open ticket priority `ticket-fix` -> repoint targetPriority null -> ticket file frontmatter becomes `priority: none`, INDEX Open row Priority cell updated, file STILL in open/, `status:` STILL Open, order.json byte-identical (unchanged).
- rehome: priority `ticket-fix` -> targetPriority 'archive-priority' -> frontmatter + INDEX cell both updated; status/file/order unchanged.
- already-at-target: repoint to the priority it already has (and separately, release a ticket already at `none`) -> { repointed: false, reason: 'already-at-target' }, zero file writes (assert ticket file + INDEX byte-identical).
- missing/closed: repoint an id that is closed, and an id that does not exist -> { repointed: false, reason: 'missing-open-ticket' }, no writes.
- no-collateral: an unrelated open ticket on another priority and a standalone ticket are byte-identical before/after a repoint of the target ticket.

ACCEPTANCE / MUST NOT BREAK
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` green (new + existing).
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core typecheck` clean.
- No edits outside write-scope.

WRITE-SCOPE: packages/core/src/tickets/**, packages/core/src/index.ts, packages/core/tests/**. Report the new/changed file paths and the exact test+typecheck command output (exit codes).

data.reason: repointTicket mutates priority frontmatter + INDEX cell only (no auto-close, order.json byte-identical), full release/rehome/no-op/missing/collateral test matrix added; core typecheck exit 0 and 624/624 tests pass.

data.task:
Atom-1 — wire the repointTicket spine into archive reconciliation so the founder's release/rehome options are one-click executable (verified-when #2). Close/option (a) already executes via requestReconciliationClose + the `reconcile-close` oz-chat command; you are adding the symmetric REPOINT lane. Model it EXACTLY on the existing close lane. Do NOT re-implement priority mutation — call `repointTicket` from @cocoder/core (shipped in atom-0).

DESIGN — one unified op + one unified command (mirror the single primitive; do not split release and rehome into two ops):

1. packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts — add `ReconciliationRepointInput { readonly workspaceId: string; readonly ticketId: string; readonly targetPriority: string | null }` and `export async function requestReconciliationRepoint(ctx, input): Promise<LaunchResult>`, structurally parallel to requestReconciliationClose (read it first and follow its shape line-for-line):
   - Resolve workspace; 404 on unknown.
   - SAME active-run-owns guard: if `ctx.inFlight.get(workspaceId)` resolves to a run whose `ticketId === input.ticketId`, refuse with 409 (`... is the target of active run ... — reconciliation repoint is refused while a run owns it`). No commit.
   - REHOME LIVENESS GUARD (this is why repoint must be governed, not hand-edited): when `targetPriority` is non-null, the target must be a LIVE priority. Check `isFile(join(workspace.path, 'cocoder/priorities/' + targetPriority + '.md'))` (same isFile helper used by assertArchivePriorityMoved/postWrapArchiveBypass). If it is not live, return 409 with a clear error (`cannot rehome ticket <id> to <target>: cocoder/priorities/<target>.md is not a live priority`) and DO NOT call repointTicket and DO NOT commit. A null target (release to standalone) skips this check.
   - Call `repointTicket({ ticketsDir: join(workspace.path,'cocoder','tickets'), repoPath: workspace.path, ticketId, targetPriority })`.
   - On `repointed:false`: NO spurious commit. Return 409 `{ ok:false, repointed:false, reason, error }` mirroring the close op's not-closed branch (reason is 'missing-open-ticket' | 'already-at-target'). already-at-target is a benign no-op — still return ok:false/repointed:false with the reason; do not 500.
   - On `repointed:true`: commit via `commitFiles(ctx.git, workspace.path, result.files, message, COCODER_GOVERNANCE_AUTHOR)` where message is `governance: reconciliation repoint ticket <id> -> <target|standalone>` (use the literal 'standalone' when targetPriority is null). 500 on commit failure mirroring the close op. Then `appendAudit(ctx.cocoderHome, { action: 'deb-reconciliation-repoint', workspaceId, ticketId, commitSha, files, targetPriority })` and `emitOzEvent(ctx, { type: 'deb-reconciliation-repoint', workspaceId })` — follow the close op's audit/event pattern; if the OzEvent type union must gain 'deb-reconciliation-repoint', add it at its owner. Return 200 `{ ok:true, repointed:true, targetPriority, commitSha, committedPaths: result.files }`. NOTE: unlike close, repoint does NOT finalize awaiting-founder runs (the ticket stays open) — do not call finalizeAwaitingFounderRunsForTicket.

2. packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts — wire the command end to end (ALL of these are required owners; a prompt/parse-only change is incomplete):
   - OzCommand union: add `{ readonly kind: 'reconcile-repoint'; readonly ticketId: string; readonly targetPriority: string | null }`.
   - OzChatReply `action.type` union: add `'reconcile-repoint'`.
   - OzChatOps interface + defaultOps: add `requestReconciliationRepoint: typeof reconciliationRepointOp` and import it alongside reconciliationCloseOp.
   - parseOzCommand: `if (verb === 'reconcile-repoint')` — `reconcile-repoint <ticketId> <standalone|priorityId>`. args[0]=ticketId, args[1]=target. Map a target of `standalone` or `none` (case-insensitive) to null; any other non-empty token is the target priorityId; missing args -> unknown with `Usage: reconcile-repoint <ticketId> <standalone|priorityId>`.
   - executeOzCommand: dispatch `command.kind === 'reconcile-repoint'` through runOp('reconcile-repoint', () => ops.requestReconciliationRepoint(ctx, { workspaceId, ticketId, targetPriority }), (out) => reconcileRepointReply(...)).
   - Add `reconcileRepointReply(ticketId, targetPriority, out)` mirroring reconcileCloseReply (failedReply on non-2xx; success names the new home or 'standalone' and the commitSha/committedPaths; action { type:'reconcile-repoint', committedPaths, commitSha }).
   - HELP_HINT: add `reconcile-repoint <ticketId> <standalone|priorityId>` next to reconcile-close.
   - archiveConfirmationReply ticketDecision text: make options (b) and (c) point at the EXECUTABLE command. Replace the vague 'through a governed path' wording so (b) reads e.g. `release to standalone: reconcile-repoint <id> standalone` and (c) reads `rehome to a live priority: reconcile-repoint <id> <priorityId>`, keeping (a) as `reconcile-close <id> <resolution>`. Keep it terse.

3. TESTS (run the affected suite, not just typecheck — governance text/contracts are behavior-pinned):
   - New packages/daemon/tests/reconciliation-repoint-op.test.ts, modeled on reconciliation-close-op.test.ts (real ticket fs + injected fake Git capturing commits). Cover: RELEASE (priority X -> null) commits with the standalone message under COCODER_GOVERNANCE_AUTHOR, ticket file frontmatter `priority: none`, still in open/, status Open, order.json unchanged; REHOME (X -> a live priority you create as cocoder/priorities/<id>.md in the fixture) commits and updates frontmatter+INDEX; REFUSES a ticket an active run owns (409, zero commits, still open at old priority); REHOME to a NON-LIVE priority is rejected 409 with zero commits and the ticket untouched; already-at-target returns repointed:false with zero commits; missing/closed ticket returns missing-open-ticket with zero commits.
   - Extend packages/daemon/tests/oz-chat.test.ts: parse `reconcile-repoint 0099 standalone` -> targetPriority null and `reconcile-repoint 0099 some-priority` -> targetPriority 'some-priority'; dispatch routes to ops.requestReconciliationRepoint with the right args (use the ops-injection style already in that test). If any existing test asserts HELP_HINT or the archive options text verbatim, update it to match.

ACCEPTANCE / MUST NOT BREAK
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` green (new + existing — especially oz-chat.test.ts, oz-args.test.ts, reconciliation-close-op.test.ts, any archive-confirmation/options assertions).
- `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck` clean.
- Do not touch the close lane's behavior, the launch queue (order.json), or core (atom-0 is frozen). No new relationship fields. No changes to Phase A/B launchability display helpers.

WRITE-SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/**, packages/daemon/tests/**. Report changed/new paths and the exact test+typecheck command output (exit codes).

data.reason: requestReconciliationRepoint mirrors the close lane (active-run guard, governance-author commit, honest already-at-target/missing reasons) with a rehome-liveness guard; reconcile-repoint wired end-to-end and archive options text now executable; daemon typecheck exit 0 and 381/381 tests pass.

data.task:
Atom-2 — docs-only alignment so the decisions owner reflects the shipped capability (no code, no test changes unless a test asserts the edited lines). The repoint spine (atom-0) and the governed release/rehome reconciliation lane + `reconcile-repoint` command (atom-1) shipped, but ADR-0041 (cocoder/decisions/0041-orchestration-ownership-and-actor-authority.md) — the single owner of Deb's actor/reconciliation authority — still enumerates ONLY the reconciliation-CLOSE authority. Record the new sibling authority there. This is documenting a shipped fact, NOT reversing a decision; do not change any normative ruling.

MAKE THESE EDITS (keep them terse and in the existing voice/format):
1. §3.2 authority list item 4 (the 'Reconciliation close' bullet, around line 172): after the close authority, add a sibling authority — Deb may also REPOINT a handled open ticket's `priority:` (RELEASE to standalone, or REHOME to a named LIVE priority) through the governed `repointTicket` spine via the `reconcile-repoint` command. Same guards as close: never a ticket an active run owns; rehome target must be a live priority (`cocoder/priorities/<id>.md`); rides `commitFiles`/the governed spine — never a raw git commit; it does NOT auto-close or touch the launch queue (`order.json`). You may add it as a new item 5 (renumbering the existing items 5/6 that follow) OR fold a one-sentence sibling clause into item 4 — choose whichever keeps the list cleanest; do not duplicate the close text.
2. The Deb row in the actor-authority table (around line 48) lists 'a reconciliation close ... via the governed closeTicket spine'. Extend that same cell to also name the reconciliation repoint (release/rehome) authority via the governed repointTicket spine, with the identical active-run guard. One added clause, not a second row.
3. If there is a closing enumeration of Deb's pinned authorities (around lines 252–271, e.g. the list ending '(e) the guarded reconcile-close authority'), add the parallel '(f) the guarded reconcile-repoint (release/rehome) authority' so the authority inventory is complete. Only if such a list exists; do not invent a new section.

CONSTRAINTS
- Edit ONLY cocoder/decisions/0041-orchestration-ownership-and-actor-authority.md. No code, no other files.
- Do not reverse, weaken, or reword any existing decision; additive clauses only.
- Match the doc's existing terminology exactly: `reconcile-repoint`, `repointTicket`, 'release to standalone', 'rehome', 'live priority'.

VERIFY (behavior-pinned-docs check)
- Grep the repo tests for any assertion of the lines you edit: `grep -rn 'reconciliation close\|closeTicket spine\|guarded reconcile-close' packages/*/tests`. If any test asserts text you changed, update that test to match and run its suite. If none do (expected — ADR-0041 body is not test-pinned), state that explicitly.
- Run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core test` to confirm no decisions/scope/governed-read test regressed.
- Report the exact diff of the ADR section, the grep result, and the test exit code.

WRITE-SCOPE: cocoder/decisions/**, and (only if a test asserts edited text) the asserting test file.

data.reason: ADR-0041 additively records the shipped reconcile-repoint release/rehome authority (table cell, §3.2 item 5 with correct renumber, closing inventory (f)) with no normative change; no test pins the edited prose; core suite 624/624 pass.
